properties.remark: PORTION OF METAL ROOF BLOWN OFF OUT-BUILDING NEAR INTERSECTION OF MONROE AND CROSSCUT ROADS.
PUBLIC IN CLOVERLAND
AT THE TRADING POST

FOUR CONFIRMED TORNADOES OCCURRED BETWEEN WILBUR AND CRESTON ON SATURDAY JUNE 6TH. THEY DEVELOPED DURING A 25 MINUTE PERIOD FROM ABOUT 250PM - 315PM. ALL OF THESE WERE NON-SUPERCELL TORNADOES. NO DAMAGE OCCURRED WITH THESE TORNADOES AS THEY REMAINED OVER WHEAT FIELDS.
WATER COVERING PORTIONS OF HIGHWAY 23 BETWEEN MILE POSTS 48 AND 53.
CARPORT AND SEVERAL TREES BLOWN INTO A POWER LINE. NEARBY OBSERVATIONS DEPICTED WIND GUSTS IN THE 35 TO 45 MPH RANGE.

STORM SURVEY ON 6/17 INDICATED MUD AND DEBRIS FLOW ACROSS ALADDIN ROAD NEAR SPIRIT FROM HEAVY RAINS ON 6/15. PUBLIC ALSO CONFIRMED THE ROAD WAS IMPASSIBLE FOR SOME TIME DURING THE EVENT. MINOR EROSION OF THE ROAD EMBANKMENT WAS ALSO NOTED APPROX. 1 MILE EAST OF SPIRIT ALONG ALADDIN ROAD.

STORM SURVEY ON 6/17 INDICATED MUD AND DEBRIS FLOW ACROSS ALADDIN ROAD NEAR SPIRIT FROM HEAVY RAINS ON 6/15. PUBLIC ALSO CONFIRMED THE ROAD WAS IMPASSIBLE FOR SOME TIME DURING THE EVENT. MINOR EROSION OF THE ROAD EMBANKMENT WAS ALSO NOTED APPROX. 1 MILE EAST OF SPIRIT ALONG ALADDIN ROAD.
1 AND 1/16TH INCH MEASURED
KETTLE FALLS INN
0.75-1.25 INCH REPORTED TO STEVENS COUNTY DISPATCH
WEATHER SPOTTER STEVENS 15 REPORTED 2.0 INCHES OF FLOWING WATER OVER DRIVEWAY
PARTIAL FLOODING ON OLD ARDEN HIGHWAY NEAR ARTMAN GIBSON ROAD
STANDING WATER ON VALLEY WESTSIDE ROAD.
AT LEAST 8 TO 9 TREES BLOWN DOWN ON STRUCTURES AND STREETS IN TOWN. ROOF DAMAGE.

NUMEROUS POWERLINES DOWN. TREE TOPS SNAPPED OFF. TREES WERE ESTIMATED 25-30 FEET HIGH AND 2-2.5 INCHES IN DIAMETER. THE TOP OF ONE TREE WAS THROWN THROUGH THE ROOFTOP OF A RV. 6 TARPS OVER THE MARINA WERE LIFTED. METAL ROOF OF THERE HOUSE WAS LIFTED UP AND KINKED.
4 TO 5 100 FOOT TREES DOWNED AND DIME SIZE HAIL.
MULTIPLE UPROOTED TREES DAMAGING AT LEAST THREE HOMES ON KALISPELL BAY OF PRIEST LAKE.

PRIEST LAKE RANGER STATION REPORTED NUMEROUS TREES DOWN ALONG ID57. PEA TO NICKEL SIZE HAIL AND DEBRIS. IN ADDITION...THERE WAS A LOSS POWER ACROSS THE AREA. LASTED 15-20 MINUTES ENDING APPROX 340 PM.
REPORTS OF TREES DOWN ACROSS VARIOUS PORTIONS OF THE TOWN.
MANY TREES TOTALLY UPROOTED. TREES FALLEN ON HOMES. DAMAGE TO UNITED CHURCH OF CHRIST IN TOWN.
SHERIFF REPORTED DOWNED TREES ACROSS CEDONIA/ADDY ROAD.
TREE BRANCHES DOWN. PEA SIZED HAIL.
SHERIFF REPORTED THE PUBLIC UTILITY COMPANY HAD TO CLEAR THE ROAD AND REPAIR POWERLINES AT LESIG RD AND LOCKE ROAD AND CLEAR DOWNED TREES.
MATURE PINES BLOWN OVER AND LYING ON HIGHWAY BETWEEN RICE AND GIFFORD.
NEIGHBORS REPORTED ROOF DAMAGE
SEVERAL UPROOTED TREES REPORTED IN THE AREA
TWO 1.5FT DIAMETER TREES UPROOTED AND FOUR 10IN DIAMETER TREES SNAPPED 15 FT UP
MATURE PINES UPROOTED ALONG HIGHWAY 31 NORTH AND SOUTH OF TOWN
MATURE PINES SNAPPED AND DOWN ALONG HIGHWAY 57 AND REEDER BAY ROAD

EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT AND LAW ENFORCEMENT FOR GRANT AND ADAMS COUNTIES. BLOWING DUST HAVE CAUSED NUMEROUS MULTI-VEHICLE ACCIDENTS WITH INJURIES. INTERSTATE 90 HAS BEEN CLOSED FROM MOSES LAKE TO RITZVILLE.
